evidences.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce celebrate Chiefs' Super Bowl win with a kiss
evidences.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce shared a kiss on the field after the Kansas City Chiefs won Super Bowl LVIII against the San Francisco 49ers in overtime. Swift touched down in Las Vegas on Sunday afternoon with actress Blake Lively and rapper Ice Spice ahead of Super Bowl LVIII and arrived at Allegiant Stadium for the big game. There she was spotted entering the stadium before heading through the San Francisco 49ers side, where her suite is located. A photo shared on social media highlighted the singer carrying a football-shaped bag with the No. 87 on it, which is, of course, Travis Kelce's number. JACKSON MAHOMES BACK IN SPOTLIGHT AS VIDEO OF HIM AT SUPER BOWL PARTY GOES VIRAL Travis Kelce kisses Taylor Swift after the NFL Super Bowl [...] Miles Teller, Lana Del Rey, Ice Spice, Taylor Swift, and Blake Lively celebrate after the Kansas City Chiefs defeated the San Francisco 49ers Miles Teller, Lana Del Rey, Ice Spice, Taylor Swift, and Blake Lively celebrate after the Kansas City Chiefs defeated the San Francisco 49ers 25-22 in overtime during Super Bowl LVIII at Allegiant Stadium on February 11, 2024 in Las Vegas, Nevada. (Michael Reaves/Getty Images) Swift joined Kelce on the fi...

evidences.content: The singer was also sporting a bejeweled necklace with an “87” Pendant—which also happens to be Travis Kelce’s number. 🚨| Taylor Swift wearing an "87" necklace in support of Travis Kelce tonight! #SuperBowlLVIII pic.twitter.com/NvinwQ8ISG Swift could be seen taking in the game from a private box alongside both her family and the Kelces—which apparently cost over $1 million, paid for in total by Travis Kelce. Social media videos showed Swift and NFL commissioner Roger Goodell in what appeared to be a deep conversation; Swift could be seen nodding as Goodell talks directly to her. In another video, Swift can be seen alongside her father, Scott, talking with Kylie Kelce, a hockey player and the wife of Kelce’s brother, Jason. evidences.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Kissed on the Field at the 2024 Super ...
evidences.content: # Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Kissed on the Field at the 2024 Super Bowl, and It Was a Real-Life Rom-Com Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Kissed on the Field at the 2024 Super Bowl and It Was a RealLife RomCom Some might have gone into Super Bowl LVIII wondering whether the Kansas City Chiefs or the San Francisco 49ers would emerge victorious, but for those of us who showed up purely for the Taylor Swift of it all, a bigger question lingered: Would Swift make it back from her Eras shows in Tokyo in time to see her boyfriend, Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce, play? Obviously, she showed up—she has a jet and a dream!!!—but even more excitingly, she and Kelce exchanged a very public (albeit fairly tasteful) kiss on the field after the Chiefs won the championship, 25-22. [...] Ever since Swift was caught on camera running offstage to kiss Kelce at a concert in Argentina last year, the couple has taken their rightful place in the annals of celebrity-PDA history. Honestly, though, it’s just...really nice to see Swift so seemingly happy and at ease after weeping along to 10 albums’ worth of her dating angst? Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Kissed on the Field at the 2024 Super Bowl and It Wa...

evidences.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce celebrate with Super Bowl kiss in Las Vegas
evidences.content: "Just when you think things couldn't get any better," Kelce said. "Being on the mountain top with my brother was something special that I'll never forget for the rest of my life. It brings me to tears even thinking about that moment ... but what we had to endure with this team. Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce celebrates with girlfriend Taylor Swift after a 25-22 win over the San Francisco 49ers in Super Bowl LVIII on Sunday at Allegiant Stadium in Las Vegas. Photo by John Angelillo/UPI "To see the guys rise to the occasion and put stats aside ... that's something special." Kelce totaled a game-high nine catches for 93 yards for the Chiefs. He will head into the off-season as a champion for the third time in the last five years. [...] "I'm on top of the world right now," Kelce told reporters after the 25-22 victory. "It's a good feeling." Swift sat in a luxury box, surrounded by fellow celebrities Blake Lively, Ice Spice and others for the majority of the game. She then made her way down to the field to celebrate the overtime victory with Kelce, his teammates and his family. Swift, who won two Grammy Awards last week, completed a 6,000-mile trip to attend the game. She...

evidences.title: Travis Kelce, Taylor Swift share celebratory kiss as fans wonder if ...
evidences.content: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift added another chapter to their love story on Sunday night as the Kansas City Chiefs star and the pop icon shared a kiss after the team’s AFC Championship victory over the Baltimore Ravens. The CBS broadcast caught Kelce and Swift kissing after Kansas City topped Baltimore, 17-10, and earned a trip to the Super Bowl for the fourth time in five years. CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM Kelce and Swift celebrate Taylor Swift looks up at Travis Kelce. (Patrick Smith/Getty Images) VIEW THE MOMENT ON X [...] VIEW THE MOMENT ON X Kelce finished with 11 catches for 116 yards and a crucial touchdown in the first quarter. He caught the ball with one hand for the game's first score. He also set a record for most postseason catches all time, surpassing Jerry Rice’s mark of 151. As the game ended, NFL fans began to speculate over whether Swift will be at Super Bowl LVIII in Las Vegas. The problem is the pop star’s travel schedule. The Eras Tour is going overseas to Japan starting on Feb. 7. Swift and company will be performing at the Tokyo Dome through Feb. 10. Sports business reporter Darren Rovell broke down how Swift would be able to attend th...

evidences.title: Final score: Chiefs triumph 25-22 in overtime, win Super Bowl LVIII
evidences.content: After being pinned at the 1-yard line, Mahomes found Kelce with space to near the first-down marker. However, the Chiefs were stuffed on the ensuing run, leading to another punt. Needing another stop from the defense, the Chiefs clamped down and got it: first down was blown up by defensive tackle Chris Jones, leading to a tackle for loss by Chenal. On the following third and long, a blitz forced a hurried throw that landed incomplete downfield. With the defense bailing the Chiefs out once again, the offense finally showed some life — especially when Mahomes ran through contact on a scramble to move the chains on third down. On the next play, he pulled a option look, taking advantage of a good fake to run 22 yards. [...] A few snaps later, the 49ers found the end zone on a quick slant, taking the lead back with a score of 16-13; the point-after attempt was blocked. Looking for a rhythm to take back the lead, the Chiefs’ offense started with a long completion to tight end Travis Kelce. Two plays later, Mahomes faced third and 10, and used a quick throw to Kelce to avoid a caving pocket and move the chains. Then, he layered a throw to the sideline, finding Justin Watson to get insi...

evidences.content: | Fumbles lost | 2 | 1 | | Penalties–yards | 6–40 | 6–55 | | Time of possession | 38:31 | 36:26 | | Turnovers | 2 | 2 | [...] The game lasted for 74 minutes and 57 seconds of game time, making it the longest Super Bowl and 7th longest NFL postseason game ever. Mahomes completed 34 of his 46 pass attempts for 333 yards with two touchdowns and one interception and was named the Super Bowl MVP for the third time in his career. He also ran for 66 yards. McCaffrey was the top rusher of the game with 22 carries for 80 yards. He also led the 49ers with eight catches for 80 yards and a receiving touchdown. evidences.title: Patrick Mahomes pulls off fantastic finish as Chiefs win Super Bowl ...
evidences.content: Mahomes put together one of the most spectacular finishes in Super Bowl history Sunday night, leading the Kansas City Chiefs on a game-tying field goal drive in the final two minutes of regulation and then a game-winning drive on their first possession of overtime. And when he hit Mecole Hardman with a 3-yard touchdown with three seconds left, the Chiefs had won Super Bowl LVIII at Allegiant Stadium, 25-22. Mahomes was spectacular at the end. He went 5-for-8 for 57 yards on the game-tying drive and then 8-for-8 for 42 yards on the game-winning drive. He also kept the drive alive by rushing eight yards for a first down on fourth-and-1 and then 19 yards on a third-and-1 that got the Chiefs to the 49ers' 13. [...] That, though, only set the stage for the wild finish, which started near the end of the third quarter on a Chiefs punt. The kick ricocheted off the left leg of 49ers cornerback Darnell Luter, forcing Ray-Ray McCloud to try to pick it up. But he couldn't, and the Chiefs recovered at the 49ers 16. One player later, Mahomes found a wide-open Marques Valdes-Scantling in the end zone, and the Chiefs had their first lead of the game at 13-10. Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce, who...

evidences.title: Chiefs defeat 49ers 25-22 in OT thriller in Super Bowl 2024. Here ...
evidences.content: Patrick Mahome with trophy after winning Super Bowl LVIII Super Bowl LVIII served as a rematch of Super Bowl LIV in 2020, when the Chiefs and Mahomes felled the Niners 31-20 to bring Kansas City its first Super Bowl in a half-century. The two head coaches who patrolled the sidelines four years ago were again at the helm Sunday. In winning, Chiefs head coach Andy Reid maintained his perfect 4-0 record against Niners coach Kyle Shanahan. The Chiefs also extended the 49ers Super Bowl drought to 30 years. "The guys were great, defense played out of their mind," Reid said. "And then the 'O' just kept persevering, and pushing and pushing." [...] Super Bowl LVIII - San Francisco 49ers v Kansas City Chiefs ## Niners take 10-0 lead on unbelievable trick play The 49ers have increased their lead to 10-0 thanks to a trick play that caught the Chiefs completely off guard. With under five minutes to play in the second quarter, Niners quarterback Brock Purdy pitched the ball back to wide receiver Jauan Jennings, who then threw it across the field to Christian McCaffrey. McCaffrey than sprinted 21 yards for the score. Jennings is the first wide receiver to throw a touchdown pass in the Super Bo...

evidences.title: Travis Kelce's Touchdown - Chiefs.com
evidences.content: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Shane Buechele throws a 51-yard pass to wide receiver Hollywood Brown. ### Jack Cochrane Recovers Pivotal Fumble on Kick Return Kansas City Chiefs linebacker Jack Cochrane recovers Dylan Laube's fumble on kick return to put Chiefs in Raiders territory. ### Travis Kelce Becomes Third Tight End in NFL History to Reach 13,000 Career Receiving Yards Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce catches a 5-yard pass from quarterback Shane Buechele to become the third tight end to reach 13,000 receiving yards. ### Chris Oladokun Connects with Travis Kelce for 5-yard Gain Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce catches a 5-yard pass from Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Chris Oladokun during the Week 18 matchup against the Las Vegas Raiders.
